name: intentional-openai
documentation:
# Intentional - OpenAI

[![Made for Intentional](https://img.shields.io/badge/made_for-intentional-blue)](https://intentional-ai.github.io/intentional/docs/home/)
[![PyPI - Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/intentional-openai.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/intentional-openai)
[![PyPI - Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/intentional-openai.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/intentional-openai)
[![Copier](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/copier-org/copier/master/img/badge/badge-grayscale-inverted-border-orange.json)](https://github.com/copier-org/copier)

Intentional plugin for OpenAI.

-----

## Table of Contents

- [Installation](#installation)
- [License](#license)

## Installation

```console
pip install intentional-openai
```

To use the Realtime API:

```
sudo apt install ffmpeg portaudio19-dev
```

## License

`intentional` is distributed under the terms of the [AGPL](LICENSE.txt) license. If that doesn't work for you, [get in touch](mailto:github@zansara.dev).

modules.name: intentional_openai.realtime_api
documentation: Client for OpenAI's Realtime API.
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2024-present ZanSara <github@zansara.dev>
SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-or-later
Inspired by
https://github.com/run-llama/openai_realtime_client/blob/main/openai_realtime_client/client/realtime_client.py

classes.name: intentional_openai.realtime_api.RealtimeAPIClient

methods.name: intentional_openai.realtime_api.RealtimeAPIClient.__init__
documentation:
A client for interacting with the OpenAI Realtime API that lets you manage the WebSocket connection, send text
and audio data, and handle responses and events.

methods.name: intentional_openai.realtime_api.RealtimeAPIClient.setup_initial_prompt
documentation: Setup initial prompt and tools. Used also after conversation end to reset the state.

methods.name: intentional_openai.realtime_api.RealtimeAPIClient.connect
documentation: Establish WebSocket connection with the Realtime API.

methods.name: intentional_openai.realtime_api.RealtimeAPIClient.disconnect
documentation: Close the WebSocket connection.

methods.name: intentional_openai.realtime_api.RealtimeAPIClient.run
documentation:
Handles events coming from the WebSocket connection.

This method is an infinite loop that listens for messages from the WebSocket connection and processes them
accordingly. It also triggers the event handlers for the corresponding event types.

methods.name: intentional_openai.realtime_api.RealtimeAPIClient.send
documentation:
Stream data to the API.

Args:
    data:
        The data chunk to stream. It should be in the format {"audio": bytes}.

methods.name: intentional_openai.realtime_api.RealtimeAPIClient.update_system_prompt
documentation: Update the system prompt to use in the conversation.

methods.name: intentional_openai.realtime_api.RealtimeAPIClient.handle_interruption
documentation:
Handle user interruption of the current response.

Args:
    lenght_to_interruption (int):
        The length in milliseconds of the audio that was played to the user before the interruption.
        May be zero if the interruption happened before any audio was played.

methods.name: intentional_openai.realtime_api.RealtimeAPIClient._update_session
documentation:
Update session configuration.

Args:
    config (Dict[str, Any]):
        The new session configuration.

methods.name: intentional_openai.realtime_api.RealtimeAPIClient._send_text_message
documentation:
Send text message to the API.

Args:
    text (str):
        The text message to send.

methods.name: intentional_openai.realtime_api.RealtimeAPIClient._send_function_result
documentation:
Send function call result back to the API.

Args:
    call_id (str):
        The ID of the function call.
    result (Any):
        The result of the function call.
async def _send_audio_message(self, audio_bytes: bytes) -> None:
Send audio data to the API.
Args:
audio_bytes (bytes):
The audio data to send.
# Convert audio to required format (24kHz, mono, PCM16)
audio = AudioSegment.from_file(io.BytesIO(audio_bytes))
audio = audio.set_frame_rate(24000).set_channels(1).set_sample_width(2)
pcm_data = base64.b64encode(audio.raw_data).decode()
# Append audio to buffer
append_event = {"type": "input_audio_buffer.append", "audio": pcm_data}
await self.ws.send(json.dumps(append_event))
# Commit the buffer
commit_event = {"type": "input_audio_buffer.commit"}
await self.ws.send(json.dumps(commit_event))

methods.name: intentional_openai.realtime_api.RealtimeAPIClient._request_response_from_llm
documentation:
Asks the LLM for a response to the messages it just received.
You need to call this function right after sending a messages that is not streamed like the audio (where the
LLM's VAD would decide when to reply instead).

methods.name: intentional_openai.realtime_api.RealtimeAPIClient._call_tool
documentation:
Calls the tool requested by the LLM.

Args:
    event (Dict[str, Any]):
        The event containing the tool call information.

A client for interacting with the OpenAI Realtime API that lets you manage the WebSocket connection, send text and
audio data, and handle responses and events.

modules.name: intentional_openai.chatcompletion_api
documentation: Client for OpenAI's Chat Completion API.
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2024-present ZanSara <github@zansara.dev>
SPDX-License-Identifier: AGPL-3.0-or-later

classes.name: intentional_openai.chatcompletion_api.ChatCompletionAPIClient

methods.name: intentional_openai.chatcompletion_api.ChatCompletionAPIClient.__init__
documentation:
A client for interacting with the OpenAI Chat Completion API.

Args:
    parent: The parent bot structure.
    intent_router: The intent router.
    config: The configuration dictionary.

methods.name: intentional_openai.chatcompletion_api.ChatCompletionAPIClient.setup_initial_prompt
documentation: Setup initial prompt and tools. Used also after conversation end to reset the state.

methods.name: intentional_openai.chatcompletion_api.ChatCompletionAPIClient.run
documentation:
Handle events from the LLM by either processing them internally or by translating them into higher-level
events that the BotStructure class can understand, then re-emitting them.

methods.name: intentional_openai.chatcompletion_api.ChatCompletionAPIClient.update_system_prompt
documentation: Update the system prompt in the LLM.

methods.name: intentional_openai.chatcompletion_api.ChatCompletionAPIClient.handle_interruption
documentation:
Handle an interruption while rendering the output to the user.

Args:
    lenght_to_interruption: The length of the data that was produced to the user before the interruption.
        This value could be number of characters, number of words, milliseconds, number of audio frames, etc.
        depending on the bot structure that implements it.

methods.name: intentional_openai.chatcompletion_api.ChatCompletionAPIClient.send
documentation: Send a message to the LLM.

methods.name: intentional_openai.chatcompletion_api.ChatCompletionAPIClient._send_message
documentation:
Generate a response to a message.

Args:
    message: The message to respond to.

methods.name: intentional_openai.chatcompletion_api.ChatCompletionAPIClient._handle_function_call
documentation: Handle a function call from the LLM.

methods.name: intentional_openai.chatcompletion_api.ChatCompletionAPIClient._route
documentation: Runs the router to determine the next system prompt and tools to use.

methods.name: intentional_openai.chatcompletion_api.ChatCompletionAPIClient._call_tool
documentation:
Call a tool with the given arguments.

Args:
    call_id: The ID of the tool call.
    function_name: The name of the tool function to call.
    function_args: The arguments to pass to the tool
A client for interacting with the OpenAI Chat Completion API.
